Authentication.description: PASETO is used for token issuance.
No hardcoded token secrets detected in source files.
Token secrets are read from environment variables.
All detected jwt.sign() calls include an expiresIn option.
Access token TTL exceeds 24 hours (e.g., "10d"). This increases the window for token misuse.
Authentication.remediation: Reduce access token TTL to 15–60 minutes. Use refresh tokens for session continuity.

Authentication.description: Password hashing uses bcrypt/argon2 with adequate salt rounds.
All route files apply isAuthenticated or are marked as public.
Role-check middleware exists and is applied to admin routes.
Logout deletes the token from the session store (Redis).
Refresh tokens are stored server-side in Redis and validated on use.

Input_Validation.description: 2 route file(s) do not appear to apply schema validation middleware: notification.route.js, health.route.js
Input_Validation.remediation: Apply Joi, Zod, or Yup schema validation middleware before every route handler that accepts user input.

Input_Validation.description: express-mongo-sanitize is registered as global middleware.
XSS sanitization middleware is applied globally.
HTTP parameter pollution protection (hpp) is registered as global middleware.
Request body size is limited (e.g., express.json({ limit: "10kb" })).
Loose Zod typing (z.any() or z.unknown()) detected in validator schemas.
Input_Validation.remediation: Replace z.any() and z.unknown() with strict type definitions on all sensitive inputs.

API_Design.description: helmet is registered as global middleware.
CORS origin has a wildcard ("*") fallback. In production this allows any origin.
API_Design.remediation: Set an explicit CORS origin without a wildcard fallback. Use an allowlist of trusted origins.

API_Design.description: Centralized error-handling middleware with 4 parameters (err, req, res, next) is registered.
404 handler is registered after all routes.
All async route handlers appear to be wrapped in try/catch or an async wrapper.
Rate limiting middleware is applied to authentication endpoints.
A global rate limiter is applied to all routes.
Controllers use semantically correct HTTP status codes.
Stack traces are conditionally excluded from error responses in production.

MongoDB.description: All Mongoose models define field-level validation.
Unique fields have unique: true index defined in the schema.
Sensitive fields (password) are excluded from query results using .select("-password") or select: false.
Pagination (limit/skip or cursor-based) is implemented for list endpoints.
No MongoDB transaction usage detected. Multi-document writes may not be atomic.
MongoDB.remediation: Use MongoDB sessions and transactions for multi-document write operations that must be atomic.

MongoDB.description: MongoDB connection string is read from an environment variable.
MongoDB connection pool settings (maxPoolSize, serverSelectionTimeoutMS) are explicitly configured.

RabbitMQ.description: Queues are declared with durable: true.
Messages are published with persistent: true (delivery mode 2).
Consumer uses manual acknowledgment (noAck: false) and calls channel.ack(msg) on success.
Dead-Letter Exchange (DLX) and Dead-Letter Queue (DLQ) are configured.
Retry mechanism with a maximum retry count is implemented.
Consumer checks for idempotency before processing (skips already-completed transactions).
RabbitMQ connection implements a reconnection strategy with a maximum attempt limit.
RabbitMQ connection errors are logged and not swallowed.

Socket.IO.description: Socket connections require authentication (token verification) before receiving application events.
Socket authentication uses the same ACCESS_SECRET as the HTTP auth middleware.
Each authenticated user is placed in an isolated room (user:<userId>).
Disconnect events are handled and the client map is cleaned up.
pingTimeout and pingInterval are explicitly configured.
Incoming socket event payloads are validated before processing.
Socket.IO CORS origin has a wildcard ("*") fallback. In production this allows any origin.
Socket.IO.remediation: Set an explicit CORS origin for Socket.IO without a wildcard fallback.

Logging.description: Log level is controlled by an environment variable (LOG_LEVEL).
No obvious sensitive field logging patterns detected.
Request logging middleware or per-request log entries are present.
Log files are written to a persistent directory with file rotation configured.

Error_Handling.description: Both process.on("uncaughtException") and process.on("unhandledRejection") handlers are registered.
Graceful shutdown closes all connections (DB, Redis, RabbitMQ) on SIGTERM and SIGINT.
Stack traces are conditionally excluded from error responses in production.
.catch() handlers are used for fire-and-forget async calls.
Balance rollback logic is present in the worker for failed payout processing.
Worker implements graceful shutdown that stops consuming before disconnecting.

Environment.description: No hardcoded secrets or connection strings detected in source files.
.env.example file exists listing required environment variables.
Required environment variables are referenced in the codebase (manual validation).
Environment.remediation: Consider using envalid or joi to validate all required env vars at startup with clear error messages.

Environment.description: NODE_ENV is referenced in the codebase to differentiate dev/production behavior.
No config validation library (envalid, joi) detected for startup environment validation.
Environment.remediation: Use envalid or joi to validate and document all required environment variables at startup.

Performance.description: Redis caching is used for frequently read data.
No synchronous blocking operations detected in request-handling code paths.
Index definitions found on model fields.
No in-memory session storage detected. Sessions appear to be stored in Redis or a database.
Worker uses a prefetch count to limit concurrent message processing.

Testing.description: "test" script is defined in package.json: "node --experimental-vm-modules node_modules/jest/bin/jest.js --runInBand".
No integration or e2e tests found (no files with "integration", "e2e", or "api" in path, and no supertest usage).
Testing.remediation: Add integration tests using supertest to cover your HTTP API endpoints.

Testing.description: No live infrastructure connection strings detected in test files without mock patterns.
Auth flow keywords (register, login, refresh, logout) are all covered in test files.

DevOps.description: No ESLint configuration found (.eslintrc, .eslintrc.js, .eslintrc.json, eslint.config.js, or "eslintConfig" in package.json).
DevOps.remediation: Add an ESLint configuration file and install eslint as a devDependency to enforce consistent code style.

DevOps.description: No Prettier configuration found (.prettierrc, .prettierrc.js, .prettierrc.json, prettier.config.js, or "prettier" key in package.json).
DevOps.remediation: Add a Prettier configuration file and install prettier as a devDependency to enforce consistent code formatting.

DevOps.description: No CI/CD pipeline file found (.github/workflows/*.yml, .gitlab-ci.yml, Jenkinsfile, .circleci/config.yml, azure-pipelines.yml).
DevOps.remediation: Add a CI/CD pipeline configuration to automate testing, linting, and deployment.

DevOps.description: No API documentation found (openapi.yaml, swagger.json/yaml, swagger-jsdoc config, or swagger/openapi source files).
DevOps.remediation: Add API documentation using OpenAPI/Swagger (e.g. swagger-jsdoc + swagger-ui-express) to improve developer experience.

DevOps.description: No CHANGELOG file found (CHANGELOG.md, CHANGELOG, or HISTORY.md).
DevOps.remediation: Add a CHANGELOG.md to document notable changes for each release, following the Keep a Changelog format.

Critical_Gaps.description: No CSRF protection detected on state-changing endpoints.
Critical_Gaps.remediation: Add CSRF protection using the csurf package or enforce SameSite=Strict on session cookies for all state-changing (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) endpoints.

Critical_Gaps.description: Request correlation IDs are present for distributed tracing.
Content-Security-Policy header is configured via helmet.
No secrets rotation documentation or automation found.
Critical_Gaps.remediation: Document a secrets rotation procedure (e.g. in README or runbook) and consider automating rotation using AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, or a scheduled rotation script.

suggested_fixes.remediation: Reduce access token TTL to 15–60 minutes. Use refresh tokens for session continuity.
